snappySummary: An absolute circus. Almiron got sent off within forty minutes for swearing at the ref. Paraguay held on for a chaotic 1-0 win.
talkingPoints: Galarza scores the opener in 64 seconds;Almiron's red card completely derailed Türkiye's defensive blueprint;Paraguay's physical display ran the clock down effectively
randomQuirk: Almiron covering his mouth to swear at the referee, only to get caught by high-definition cameras.

snappySummary: Morocco danced through the Scottish midfield and claimed a deserved 2-1 victory. Scotland's late surge was too little, too late.
talkingPoints: Morocco's liquid football carved open the Scottish double-decker bus;Conceded to an overhead kick that defied all laws of Scottish gravity;Morocco's defense stood tall under late aerial pressure
randomQuirk: A Scottish fan in a kilt spotted reading a tactical guidebook upside-down in the 88th minute.

snappySummary: The USA cruised to a 3-0 victory over Australia. The Socceroos looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park.
talkingPoints: Pulisic was a walking cheat code, scoring two and assisting one;Australia completely bottled it in possession, committing 18 turnovers;The high press from the Americans left Australia gasping for air
randomQuirk: An Australian defender trying to clear the ball, only to kick it directly into his own teammate's face.

snappySummary: Brazil dominated Haiti 5-0 in a match that felt like a training session. Vinicius Jr scored a hat-trick of pure class.
talkingPoints: Vinicius Jr put on a show with three goals and endless flair;Haiti's Sunday League defending conceded three goals from simple corners;Brazil hit the post three times, preventing an even bigger scoreline
randomQuirk: A Haitian defender asking Vinicius Jr to swap shirts during a water break in the 30th minute.

editionTitle: Dutch Delight and Swedish Defensive Disasters
snappySummary: A masterclass in clinical finishing from the Dutch, while Sweden managed to lose 5-1 despite actually having more shots on target. Absolute Sunday League stuff at the back from the Swedes.
talkingPoints: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo running riot with two goals apiece, making the Swedish defence look like they were having a casual kickabout in the park.;Sweden's bizarre efficiency in reverse: 16 shots and 8 on target, yet they leave with their tails between their legs after conceding five.;Crysencio Summerville's eventful shift: coming on at halftime, setting up Gakpo, scoring a lovely fifth, and then getting injured in stoppage time.
randomQuirk: Sweden managed to pick up three yellow cards through Gudmundsson, Ayari, and Bergvall, but couldn't find a way to stop the Dutch from scoring with almost every shot they took.

editionTitle: Undav Saves the Day After German Defensive Wobbles
snappySummary: Germany looked to have completely bottled it after Franck Kessie put Côte d'Ivoire ahead, but super-sub Deniz Undav spared their blushes with a brilliant second-half brace.
talkingPoints: Deniz Undav coming off the bench in the 60th minute and turning the game on its head with goals in the 68th and 94th minutes.;Nico Schlotterbeck's early injury forcing Antonio Rüdiger into action at halftime, disrupting the German backline.;Côte d'Ivoire's heartbreak after defending resolutely with zero yellow cards, only to concede in the dying seconds of injury time.
randomQuirk: Despite a highly competitive and physical battle, the referee didn't brandish a single yellow card for either side over the entire 90 minutes.
